Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. 2026-03-12T03:18:39.5505937Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T03:17:23.528738191Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":2,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T03:17:49.411846452Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":56998,\"title\":\"[FEATURE REQ] Support Key Vault REST API version 2026-02-01 in Azure.Security.KeyVault.*\",\"body\":\"### Library name\\n\\nAzure.Security.KeyVault.*\\n\\n### Please describe the feature.\\n\\n### Feature request\\n\\nPlease add support for Key Vault REST API version `2026-02-01` in the `Azure.Security.KeyVault.*` client libraries (`Secrets`, `Keys`, and `Certificates`).\\n\\n### Background\\n\\nMicrosoft Learn now documents the Key Vault access control default change for API version `2026-02-01` and later.  \\nAccording to the documentation, new vaults created with `2026-02-01` or later default to Azure RBAC, and older API versions are planned to be retired by **2027-02-27**.\\n\\nAt the moment, the current `main` branch of the .NET Key Vault client libraries still appears to use `2025-07-01` as the latest supported service version, for example:\\n\\n- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets.SecretClientOptions`\\n- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys.KeyClientOptions`\\n- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Certificates.CertificateClientOptions`\\n\\n### Why this matters\\n\\nApplications and tooling that need to align with the new Key Vault API behavior cannot explicitly target `2026-02-01` yet via the .NET SDK.\\n\\nSupport for `2026-02-01` would help with:\\n- validating behavior against the new default access control model\\n- preparing applications before older API versions are retired\\n- avoiding custom or workaround-based REST calls outside the SDK\\n\\n### Requested change\\n\\nPlease add support for Key Vault REST API version `2026-02-01` to the relevant `Azure.Security.KeyVault.*` libraries and expose it through the corresponding client options.\\n\\nIf full support is not planned yet, could you share the expected timeline / ETA or whether there is an existing tracking issue or PR for this work?\\n\\n### References\\n\\n- [Microsoft Learn: “Prepare for Key Vault API versions 2026-02-01 and later”](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/access-control-default?tabs=azure-cli)\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56998\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"natori-tatsuya\",\"id\":107160024,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/natori-tatsuya\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/107160024?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-12T03:15:51Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-12T03:15:51Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-12T03:18:39.5744751Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T03:18:20.958764615Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a feature request to add support for Key Vault REST API version `2026-02-01` in the `Azure.Security.KeyVault.*` client libraries (Secrets, Keys, and Certificates). The requester notes the current latest supported API version in the SDK is `2025-07-01`, and the new `2026-02-01` version introduces an important behavioral change (new vaults default to Azure RBAC). Older API versions are planned for retirement by **2027-02-27**.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Type:** Feature request  \n**Affected libraries:**\n- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets`\n- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys`\n- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Certificates`\n\n**Key motivation:**\n- API version `2026-02-01` changes the default access control model for new Key Vault vaults (defaults to Azure RBAC instead of Vault Access Policies)\n- The SDK currently exposes only up to `2025-07-01` in `*ClientOptions` service version enums\n- Retirement of older API versions is planned for 2027-02-27, creating urgency\n\n**No duplicate found** — this appears to be the first open issue specifically tracking `2026-02-01` support.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Relevant Code Locations\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe service version enums to update would be in:\n- `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/src/SecretClientOptions.cs`\n- `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/src/KeyClientOptions.cs`\n- `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Certificates/src/CertificateClientOptions.cs`\n\nEach of these files contains a `ServiceVersion` enum where the new `V2026_02_01` entry (and corresponding default) would need to be added, along with any new API surface exposed by the new version.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Confirm that the Key Vault REST API `2026-02-01` spec is published and stable (check [azure-rest-api-specs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/main/specification/keyvault))\n- [ ] Update `ServiceVersion` enum in `SecretClientOptions`, `KeyClientOptions`, and `CertificateClientOptions` to add `V2026_02_01`\n- [ ] Update the default service version to `2026-02-01` (or decide on policy — may want to keep old default for back-compat)\n- [ ] Regenerate code if the libraries use code generation from the OpenAPI/TypeSpec spec\n- [ ] Update CHANGELOG.md for each affected library\n- [ ] Add or update tests for new API version behavior (particularly RBAC default)\n- [ ] Review whether any new properties/operations are introduced in `2026-02-01` that need SDK surface\n- [ ] Assess whether a new package version (minor bump) is needed\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Helpful References\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [MS Learn: Prepare for Key Vault API versions 2026-02-01 and later](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/access-control-default?tabs=azure-cli)\n- [Key Vault REST API specs (azure-rest-api-specs)](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/main/specification/keyvault/data-plane)\n- [SDK contributing
